trivia_bullets.text: Ugin the Spirit Dragon was slain on Tarkir by Nicol Bolas roughly 1,280 years before the events of Khans of Tarkir; his death ended the elemental storms that produced Tarkir's dragons, leading to their extinction. Sarkhan Vol, the planeswalker protagonist of the Tarkir block, was guided to Ugin's tomb by a vision, then traveled back in time (in Fate Reforged) to prevent Ugin's death. The Tomb of the Spirit Dragon card depicts the literal site where Ugin's bones lay [Wizards: 'The Story of Tarkir Block'; 'A Tarkir of Dragons'].

Flavor text: "The voice calls me here, yet I see only bones. Is this more dragon trickery?" — Sarkhan Vol. The quote captures Sarkhan at the moment of discovery: guided by Ugin's psychic call across centuries, he arrives expecting a living dragon and finds only a tomb. His accusation of 'dragon trickery' reflects his fractured mental state during KTK's narrative [Scryfall].

EDHREC rank 1,675 indicates moderate Commander adoption. The card's niche is colorless-creature-heavy builds — its activated ability reads '{2}, {T}: You gain 1 life for each colorless creature you control', which scales with Eldrazi, artifact creatures, and golem tribal strategies. It also produces colorless ({C}) mana on a plain tap, making it a zero-cost slot in colorless commander decks running Ugin, Kozilek, or Emrakul as generals [Scryfall; EDHREC: Tomb of the Spirit Dragon page].

Illustrated by Sam Burley (same artist as Sandsteppe Citadel in this set). The card has no reprints — sole printing is KTK. Scryfall confirms this is Burley's second KTK land entry in this wave, both in the large-landscape style he is known for [Scryfall].
